strengths: TypeScript and Node.js fullstack experience is directly relevant to the JD's core stack requirements, with multi-year commercial use across several employers.
Exposure to React (Optilyz), REST/GraphQL APIs, and AWS CDK provides partial coverage of the JD's required and nice-to-have skills.
Long career with diverse project types (web, mobile, embedded, GIS, AI tooling) demonstrates adaptability and broad technical literacy.
concerns: Candidate holds Senior/Staff-level titles throughout a 14-year career — this is a Junior/Mid role, creating a strong mismatch in seniority expectations, compensation norms, and likely retention risk.

No demonstrable experience with PostgreSQL or relational databases in a production context; the resume lists MongoDB and MySQL but the JD specifically prefers PostgreSQL and relational database familiarity.

No exposure to the domain (renewable energy, building renovation, energy consulting, contractor/CRM workflows) and no evidence of working on customer/contractor portal products or data integration pipelines of the type described.

key_factors.factor: No PostgreSQL or relational DB production evidence

response.justification: The candidate is a 14-year veteran with Staff and Senior titles at every employer, making them a structural misfit for an entry-level individual contributor role; the gap in seniority alone raises serious concerns about motivation, compensation alignment, and whether they would stay once a more appropriate opportunity appeared. Beyond the seniority problem, the resume shows no PostgreSQL experience in a production setting (MongoDB and DLang dominate), no NestJS usage, and no work in energy consulting, renovation workflows, or CRM/document-management integration — the specific domain knowledge and toolchain the JD centers on. Listing React only at Optilyz alongside MongoDB (not PostgreSQL) means even the partial stack overlap is weaker than it first appears. A candidate at this level would require significant downward adjustment in scope and autonomy that is unlikely to serve either party well.
